title: coordinator/task_queue: CycleDetected and UnknownTask Display strings use Debug formatting for TaskId
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/coordinator/task_queue.rs:99-105 (TaskQueueError)
## Violation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum TaskQueueError {
    #[error("task {missing:?} is not in the queue")]
    UnknownTask { missing: TaskId },
    #[error("adding dependency {from:?} → {to:?} would form a cycle")]
    CycleDetected { from: TaskId, to: TaskId },
}
```

The `{...:?}` Debug formatter is used in user-facing error messages, producing output like `"task TaskId(42) is not in the queue"`. The `TaskId(...)` wrapping is implementation noise — users want `42`. `TaskId` is a newtype around `u64` with a public `raw()` accessor; a `Display` impl would be one line.

## Mandated refactor
Implement `impl fmt::Display for TaskId { fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result { write!(f, "{}", self.0) } }`. Change the error format strings to `{missing}` / `{from} → {to}`. Drop the `:?` everywhere TaskId is rendered for end users.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
